title: Saccoloma domingense
intro: Saccoloma domingense is a species of fern, belonging to the family Saccolomataceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Saccoloma domingense include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
taxonomy: Saccoloma domingense is a species of fern in the family Saccolomataceae. It belongs to the class Polypodiopsida, which includes most modern ferns. The order to which it belongs is Polypodiales, a group of ferns that are known for their diverse range of species. At the family level, Saccoloma domingense is part of the family Saccolomataceae, which is a small family of ferns. This species is native to the Dominican Republic and other parts of the Caribbean.
